"Sec. 1  
[The given equation is a combination of a quartic term and an absolute value function. To analyze the number of real solutions, it is helpful to consider the behavior of the function on different intervals based on the absolute value. Since the absolute value function behaves differently for positive and negative inputs, we can split the analysis into two cases: when the input is non-negative and when it is negative.]  

Sec. 2  
[By splitting the equation into cases and analyzing the resulting functions, it can be shown that the equation has exactly four real number solutions. This is because the function increases without bound as the input grows in magnitude, and the absolute value term ensures symmetry about the y-axis, leading to two solutions on each side of the y-axis.]"
